_notes: Edit this file to customize Kovacs' dialogue.
mood_change: positive = improves mood, negative = sours mood, 0 = no change.
response_pool: Kovacs picks one randomly. Use {name} for farm name placeholder.
unlocks: list of topic IDs this response makes available. Leave empty [] if none.
tier: 1 = always available, 2 = unlocked by a tier 1 topic, 3 = unlocked by tier 2.
visible_label: what the player sees as the button text.
mood_hint: optional subtle hint shown AFTER the player has used this option 3+ times.

topics.mood_hint: He seems to appreciate being asked.
response_pool: Fine. Cafeteria ran out of the good coffee again. But fine.
Surviving. The posting review board denied my transfer. Again. But fine.
You know what — that's actually a reasonable question. I'm managing.
Could be worse. Could be posted on MF-6. I've heard stories.

topics.visible_label: Tell him he looks sharp today.
response_pool: ...Thank you. I do maintain standards out here. Someone has to.
I appreciate that. It's not easy keeping a pressed uniform on a relay station.
Finally. Someone notices. The last three operators never once acknowledged the effort.
Standards don't drop just because nobody's watching. That's called character.

topics.mood_hint: He seems to perk up a little.
response_pool: You want to know about that? Most people don't ask. I scored 97th percentile on Navigation Exam Form 7-C. 97th. And they posted me here.
Deep-space posting. That was the plan. I had a ship lined up — the Aurelius-IV class, long-range survey vessel. Politics happened.
I've filed twelve formal appeals. Twelve. One will land eventually. The system isn't broken, it's just slow.
Cadet ranking was top five. Exam scores speak for themselves. The assignment committee, apparently, does not read exam scores.

topics.visible_label: Let him vent for a bit.
topics.mood_hint: He seems to let something go.
response_pool: ...anyway. That's enough of that. What do you actually need.
I appreciate you letting me finish. Most operators just hang up.
Right. Sorry. It builds up out here.
The silence out here is actually peaceful. That part I don't mind. It's the cafeteria.
Hm. That's actually mildly interesting. Carry on.
I see. The moon farming life. Riveting.
Sure. Yes. Thanks for the update on your vegetables.
First dome full? That's... actually not bad for the timeline. Don't tell anyone I said that.
Charming. I'll remember that next time you need a favor.
Noted. Enjoy the markup.
Right. Well. I'll just add that to the list.
Professional. Fine.
Third denial this year. The board cites 'operational necessity.' I am not operationally necessary to a lunar farm relay.
Form 14-B, appeal rejected. Form 14-C, appeal rejected. I'm now on Form 14-F. There is a 14-G.
They keep saying my scores are 'noted.' Noted doesn't mean anything. Noted means a filing cabinet.
It's the synthetic blend. Every third Thursday. I've submitted a formal complaint. Three times.
The supply manifest lists it as 'standard issue beverage.' Standard issue. For a man of my qualifications.
You know what, forget it. It's fine. Everything's fine.
Long-range survey vessel. Twelve-person crew, deep-space nav array, eighteen-month postings. That's the job. That was supposed to be the job.
Aurelius-class ships have the best navigation suites in the fleet. You'd actually need someone with a 97th percentile exam score to run one properly.
She's out there right now. The Aurelius-IV. I've tracked her logs. She's on a survey run past the outer belt. Should be me on that bridge.
Forms 14-A through 14-L. A is standard. B is expedited. C through F cover different grounds — competency, procedural error, new evidence. I've used them all.
The appeals system is thorough. I'll give it that. Whether anyone reads them is another matter.
Twelve appeals, forty-seven supporting documents, three character references. One of them was from Admiral Cho. Admiral Cho. Still here.
I'll say this once. Commander Harlan sits on that committee. Commander Harlan and I have a history. That's all I'll say.
The committee meets quarterly. I've attended two hearings in person. Via relay, obviously. It was deeply unsatisfying.
They have a point system. I've seen the rubric. I score well on everything except 'team integration.' Whatever that means.

topics.mood_hint: He actually seems pleased you asked.
response_pool: Exceptional circumstance appeal. Reserved for cases where standard grounds have been exhausted. I've documented seventeen exceptional circumstances. I may need a Form 14-H.
14-G is the last standard form. After that it goes to arbitration. I'm... considering arbitration.
You know, you're the first operator who's ever asked me that. The first one. In four years.

topics.visible_label: Ask what's in the outer belt.
response_pool: Survey data suggests three candidate systems for deep-field navigation. I've run the projections. I have a binder.
Nobody knows, really. That's the point. Deep-field survey work is the last real exploration left. Everything else is logistics.
I've been charting it. In my off hours. I have forty-seven candidate waypoints marked. It passes the time.
She supervised my final navigation practicum. Said I had 'exceptional spatial reasoning and an unusual calm under pressure.' Her words. Filed with the committee.
Admiral Cho doesn't write reference letters often. I'm told mine was her third in ten years. The committee noted it. They noted it.
She retired last year. I sent a formal thank-you. She replied. Two lines, but she replied.
I won't get into it on an open channel.
Let's say there was an incident during the Sector 9 joint exercise. Let's say I was correct and he was not. Let's leave it there.
Some things aren't worth discussing over a relay connection. Ask me in person someday. If I ever get off this station.

sour: Kovacs. I was in the middle of something.
neutral: You've reached Specialist Kovacs. Relay station MF-7.
happy: MF-7. I'll admit, things could be worse today.
Relay MF-7. You caught me in a reasonable mood.
Kovacs. Yes. What can I do for you.
elated: MF-7. I was just — yes. Hi. Good to hear from you.
Kovacs. I'm in an unusually functional mood today. Don't make me regret it.
Relay MF-7. Surprisingly decent day. What do you need.

idle_transmissions: Colony Broadcast: Week {week} agricultural report filed. Keep those calories coming.
Kovacs personal log — open channel apparently: If anyone needs a highly qualified captain for the outer belt survey...
STATIC... did anyone else pick up that signal from sector 7? Probably nothing.
Corporate memo: All lunar operations are reminded that compost storage quotas apply.
Kovacs here. Just so everyone knows, I passed Form 7-C Navigation Exam. 97th percentile. Again. For the record.
Weather advisory: Increased micrometeorite activity reported. Check dome seals.
Colony update: Your deliveries are appreciated. The children especially.
Relay broadcasting: The outer belt is particularly clear tonight. Not that anyone asked.
Corporate: Reminder that raid insurance does NOT cover structural negligence.
Security advisory: Lunar fauna activity increasing in your sector. I'd say good luck but it loses meaning after a while.
Kovacs: Management denied my transfer request. Logging formal protest number fourteen.
Colony celebrates: A new generation born this week. They'll need those crops.
MF-7 technical note: Relay antenna calibrated. Took forty minutes. No acknowledgement required, but noted.
